⚔ Franchise Login

Sign in to load your franchise ledger, or create a new account to start a fresh company.

Cloud saves are tied to the account you log into.

📒 Recent Transactions
✓ Saved
✦ Active Contracts
↑ Gold per progress tick (max 100 gp per task)
Action row: ▼ down, ▲ up, ⇡ top, 🕒 defer, 🗃 archive, +gp claim gold.
✦ Contract Archive

⭐ = favourite (daily/custom auto-activates at morning reset). Weekly, monthly, and yearly tasks can be scheduled with Pick selectors and move to Contracts at the configured start of day. ↑↓ reorder within group. Action icons: ⇡ move to top, 🪙 claim gold, ▶ activate, 🗑 delete.

✦ The Adventuring Party
✦ Recruitment Office
Review candidates, hirelings, or cancel to return to the Party screen.
Standard Recruitment

Choose from five candidates. First roll is free — additional rerolls cost 10 gp.

Roll the dice to reveal candidates…
🪓 Hire a Hireling

Low-cost labour with minimal abilities. Cannot go on quests alone. First roll is free — rerolls cost 5 gp.

Roll to find available labour…
⭐ Custom Mercenary

Hand-pick attributes for a higher fee. Each selected attribute adds 50 gp to the base cost. Skills capped at 4, tools at 2, languages at 2 — same as standard, but you choose which ones. Comes with a class-appropriate equipment kit.

You may pick up to 4 skill pills. Leave all unselected to randomize class-appropriate skills.
You may pick up to 2 tool pills. Leave all unselected to randomize tools.
You may pick up to 2 language pills. Common is always included automatically.
🪙 500 gp base
Base 500 gp + 0 gp in options = 500 gp total

✦ The Wandering Emporium

Rate: 5 QP = 1 gp · Balance: 0 QP

Relentless Quest
Relentless Quest
Version 1.641
Dev mode: navy quick-access bar below the header with Ledger, Content Editor, version number, and Exit Dev Mode.
Branding
Daily Reset
Use an exact time. Favourite contracts auto-activate once each day the first time the app reaches or passes that time.
📜 Change Log
Tap to review notable stable updates.
Version 1.641 — Pet prereq fixes: (1) Purchase button suppressed for pet slots (pets aren't in the store). (2) qddCharCoversSlot now lights up the pet's owner char as a placement candidate. (3) Unowned/squad pets still allow any char to satisfy the slot.
Version 1.640 — Prereq slot classification fix: Bag of Holding now classified as an asset at slot-build time (nonChar=true, isAsset=true) so it renders as a prereq tile rather than a char slot. Any pet name (including char-owned party pets) classified as isPet=true at slot-build time. Pet prereq tile turns green when the owning char is placed in a peg slot.
Version 1.639 — Prereq precision fixes: (1) Bag/squad assets satisfy the nonChar prereq SLOT only — no longer marks individual char peg-cards. (2) Char-owned pets require that char to be placed in a peg slot; unowned/squad pets count freely. Gear prereqs restored to char-inv only.
Version 1.638 — Prereq fixes: (1) Bag of Holding now recognized as an asset prereq (routes to partyAssets+storage, not char inv). (2) Pet prereqs check all party pets, not just peg-slot char's pet. (3) Gear prereqs also check squad partyAssets so shared party items count. (4) isPetItemName checks partyAssets entries for char-owned pets. (5) qddCharCoversSlot checks squad assets so dragging any char satisfies asset/bag prereq slots.
Version 1.637 — Three bag-of-holding fixes: (1) sqSave now searches all bags not just unowned ones. (2) Squad deletion uses _isBagOfHolding() instead of exact name "Bag of Holding" so Longinus variants are returned to storage. (3) On load, bag ownerRefs pointing to renamed/deleted squads are cleared so franchiseHasBagOfHolding can find them.
Version 1.636 — Bug fix: franchiseHasBagOfHolding() was excluding squad-assigned bags (ownerType==='squad'). Both franchise bags are assigned to squads so the 50 gp enrollment route was never unlocking. Squad-owned bags now count.
Version 1.635 — Bag of Holding recovery: after sbPull, if franchiseStorage contains a bag-of-holding name but partyAssets is missing it (lost in a failed push), the bag is automatically restored to partyAssets. sbPush party_assets insert failures now log a visible warning instead of silently losing data.
Version 1.634 — Quest Map Collect All now sweeps both completed regular quests and completed grind missions. Grind collection uses the full gcollect flow: 90/10 split, char coin purses, item purchases, quest log entry, and immediate sbPush.
Version 1.633 — Root & Branch capstone reward set to "Longinus Carpetbag of Holding" (bagOfHolding:true so it counts for the 50 gp squad enrollment route).
Version 1.632 — Squad swap: characters currently on an active quest, grind, or downtime job are now excluded from all swap candidate pools (symmetric squad↔squad, asymmetric unassigned↔squad, and the unassigned virtual squad). Added _buildBusySet() helper.
Version 1.631 — New QP features: (a) Quest Map Collect All fast-forwards all naturally-completed quests silently with a summary. (b) QP-to-GP icon above store search with 10/25/50/75/100%% pill options; also on unmet gold prereq peg-slots in quest assignment. (c) Short Rest and Early Completion on in-progress quest cards: auto-offered based on elapsed fraction (1/3, 1/2, 3/4+), temporary payout continues quest, early completion ends it. (d) startedAt timestamp on quest pending entries. All costs/rates configurable in QP Manager.
Version 1.630 — Carousel arrows now overlap viewport by half their width: hole carousel margin 32px→14px, char carousel margin 30px→13px.
Version 1.629 — Quest assignment peg slots: changed from 1x4 to 2x3 grid (2 columns, 3 rows per page). Viewport adjusted to 260px.
Version 1.628 — quest_prereqs DB table support: loadContentLibrary now fetches structured prereqs and attaches as q._prereqs[]. questPrereqCheck and qddBuildInfoPrereqs use _prereqs when present, falling back to q.pre blob for older quests. Editor saves/loads prereqs to quest_prereqs table.
Version 1.627 — Fix peg slot viewport: qhole-car-track and qhole-car-page now have height:100% so repeat(4,1fr) rows divide the viewport correctly. Removed min-height:64px floor that was causing overflow. Removed translateY carousel offset that caused mobile overlap.
Version 1.626 — QP manager: added configurable costs for (a) cancel/recall quest (costRecall, default 25), (b) advance downtime per job (costDowntime, default 5), (c) grind auto-assign (costGrindAuto, default 50). All three were previously hardcoded.
Version 1.625 — (1) Peg slots: 1x4 per page, second page has 4 optional slots. (2) Party carousel lifted up by half a slot height.
Version 1.624 — Fix: qddCharCoversSlot now checks actual c.inv for item slots instead of returning true for any char. Hirelings and chars without the required item in inventory will no longer show a match tag or be accepted into an item peg slot. Removed redundant itemTags block in qddRenderPeg — matchedSlots now drives all tags consistently.
Version 1.623 — Fix: Personal items (🔒) section now self-heals — if an item is in c.ownedItems but missing from c.inv (e.g. after a migration or DB round-trip), it is re-added to inv so it appears in the card. migrateMisroutedAssets now also preserves ownedItems tagging when moving character-personal assets from partyAssets to c.inv.
Version 1.622 — (1) Peg slot viewport expanded to 360px showing 5 slots per page. (2) Collapsible header panel: replaced animated card carousel with a flat 1x3 column of panels (Description, Details, Best Fit) that stretch to full content height when expanded.
Version 1.621 — (1) Gear item peg slots now accept character placement — qddCharCoversSlot returns true for item type, and only pet/asset items remain nonChar status tiles. (2) Party carousel hugs the button row via margin-top:auto in a flex-column scroll container.
Version 1.620 — (1) Item prereq slot now only shows met when a char carrying the item is placed in a peg slot — not just any squad/party member. (2) Peg slots reduced to 1x4 with second page right. (3) Quest modal scroll locked vertically unless accessibility font is set above Normal (18px).
Version 1.619 — Quest modal: (1) Fixed collapsible title panel — removed overflow:hidden which was clipping the absolute overlay. (2) Restored 1x6 peg slot grid. (3) Modal expanded to 96vh to use nearly full screen height.
Version 1.618 — Quest modal fine-tuning: (1) Expanded title panel overlaps lower content instead of pushing it down. (2) Level prereq is now a real drop-target peg slot accepting chars of sufficient level. (3) Unmet item prereq slots show a Purchase button linking to the store. (4) Peg slot grid changed to 1x5.
Version 1.617 — Quest modal redesign: (1) Title/cards collapsed into a closed details bar by default. (2) Level, gold, item, pet, mount prereqs now appear as nonchar status tiles in peg slot area with live checkmarks. (3) Peg slots: 1 column x 6 rows, extra slots page right. (4) Body font sizes increased. (5) Button row hugs bottom.
Version 1.616 — Comprehensive prereq check rewrite. questPrereqCheck and questPrereqHtml now use consistent logic: skills/tools/lang/faith from all peg-slot chars; item gear/weapon from non-busy peg-slot c.inv only; vehicle/building items from partyAssets+storage; pet items from pet assets owned by non-busy squad members. Level prereq now excludes busy chars. Added isAssetItemName() and isPetItemName() helpers for routing.
Version 1.615 — Fix: 🎒 Longsword contribution showing for chars outside the active squad. buildQuestContributionHtml now checks whether the char is a member of the active cycle squad before showing item contributions. If no squad is assigned to the cycle, all chars are eligible as before.
Version 1.614 — (1) Level-up in quest modal now calls updateQuestModal() after re-rendering so the prereq check and Begin button refresh immediately. (2) buildQuestContributionHtml [indicator a] now respects isBusy for item contributions — busy chars no longer show 🎒 item match. (3) Carousel item tags [indicator c] now filters busy chars so item match tags only show for available chars. All three prereq indicators now agree.
Version 1.613 — Auto-migrate misrouted gear assets: weapons/armor/gear that ended up in partyAssets (due to old party:true bug on all DB items) are automatically moved to the correct character's c.inv on pull. Unowned gear assets move to franchise storage. Runs once after sbPull completes.
Version 1.612 — Fix prereq: busy chars (on another quest) can no longer satisfy item prerequisites. questPrereqCheck now builds a busy set from questState.pending and excludes busy char inventories from the item inv pool. Also fixed qddSyncCheckboxes to never check busy chars in peg slots. Skills/tools/lang prereqs still count all assigned chars since proficiencies exist regardless of current quest assignment.
Version 1.611 — Fix 3 bugs: (1) Prereq check counting busy chars: getQuestChars now also checks questState.pending to exclude busy chars even if their checkbox is still enabled. (2) Sell price 1gp: mergeCustomContent was resetting GEAR to built-ins, wiping DB items like Longsword. DB items now stored in DB_GEAR/DB_VEHICLES/DB_BUILDINGS caches and re-applied after every mergeCustomContent via applyDBItemsToArrays(). (3) Item not leaving inventory: same root cause as (2) — GEAR lookup failed so item type routing broke. Fixed by same applyDBItemsToArrays mechanism.
Version 1.610 — Fix: quest prereq check incorrectly passing when a busy character was previously assigned to the quest. Disabled+checked checkboxes were still read by querySelectorAll. Fixed in two places: getQuestChars now uses :not(:disabled) selector, and _prevAssigned filters out busy characters so they are never pre-checked.
Version 1.609 — Fix: weapons, armor, and gear purchased from store were routing to party assets panel instead of character inventory. loadItemsFromDB was setting party:true on ALL items. Now only vehicles and buildings get party:true — gear/weapons/armor/magic go to c.inv as intended.
Version 1.608 — Remove party debug logs. DB duplicates confirmed to be from a different user account (12080719…) — existing rows cleaned via SQL, push lock prevents future duplicates.
Version 1.607 — Fix duplicate party/squad rows: sbPush runs all operations in Promise.all (parallel), so concurrent pushes race — both delete, then both insert, creating duplicates. Added _pushInProgress lock so only one push runs at a time. Concurrent push attempts are silently dropped. Also cleans up stale duplicate rows already in the DB.
Version 1.606 — Fix: squads, characters, and icons showing stale localStorage data. Root cause: initSession was monkey-patched to call loadContentLibrary(false) immediately after starting, without waiting for sbPull to finish. This caused loadContentLibrary's internal renderAdv() to fire with stale S.squads from localStorage before sbPull had rebuilt S from the database. Monkey-patch removed — loadContentLibrary is already called inside initSession's sbPull chain.
Version 1.605 — Fix: renamed items not updating in store. loadItemsFromDB was merging DB rows over built-ins by name — so renaming an item created a duplicate instead of replacing the original. DB is now the sole source of truth: when DB rows exist for a category, they replace the built-in array entirely. Built-ins only serve as fallback when DB has zero rows.
Version 1.604 — Fix: franchise push was doing INSERT instead of UPSERT, causing a 409 duplicate key error on subsequent saves. Now uses UPSERT on user_id so the existing row is updated rather than re-inserted.
Version 1.603 — Items system: loadItemsFromDB() added. Fetches all published items from the items table (joined with effects via item_effects), merges into GEAR/VEHICLES/BUILDINGS arrays. Effects mapped to legacy item.effect format for full backward compatibility. Called on startup, login, and Pull.
Version 1.602 — Fix squad member count showing 0: loadContentLibrary was running in parallel with sbPull and calling renderAdv() before sbPull finished populating S.party. Now sbPull runs first, then content and pets load sequentially after, so S.party is always populated before any render.
Version 1.601 — Fix: SQUAD_ROLES and ROLE_KITS were accidentally deleted during pet migration (they lived between PETS and QUEST SETS). Restored.
Version 1.600 — Fix crash: BUILTIN_VEHICLES and BUILTIN_BUILDINGS were accidentally removed when BUILTIN_PETS was deleted during pet migration. Restored both snapshot variables so mergeCustomContent can reset vehicles and buildings to built-in state on each call.
Version 1.599 — Fix pets not showing in store: loadPetsFromDB was bailing early when uid() was null (session not yet restored, or guest). Now uses plain fetch with anon key — RLS already restricts to published=true for unauthenticated requests. Also called on guest boot.
Version 1.598 — Fix: pets not showing in store. loadPetsFromDB was never called on startup — only on manual Pull. Now called in initSession alongside sbPull and loadContentLibrary so pets load immediately on login.
Version 1.597 — Pet system migrated from JS array + content_items JSON to dedicated Supabase pets table. PETS is now loaded via loadPetsFromDB() from the pets table directly. No more BUILTIN_PETS, tombstones, or three-way merge logic. published column is a single boolean — true = in store, false = hidden.
Version 1.596 — Fix pet tombstone suppression: mergeCustomContent was filtering _deleted pets out of allPets before the suppression check could run, so tombstones never removed built-in pets from PETS. Fixed by running the suppression check first, then filtering. Also fixed editor: built-in pets now show as Published by default (they are in the store);
Version 1.595 — Fix: content_items visibility enforcement. loadContentLibrary was fetching ALL rows with no filter, so private content (e.g. unpublished pets) was visible to every user on every device. Now only fetches shared/official rows for everyone, plus private rows owned by the current user. Unpublishing an item now correctly hides it from other users' stores and sessions.
Version 1.594 — Content editor collapse/expand rebuilt with event delegation: single click listener on the list element handles toggle, reorder, and row selection in the correct priority order. Toggle no longer fights with row selection handler. Collapse state initialized after Supabase pull so custom cycles are visible. Quest reorder logic simplified.
Version 1.593 — Content editor full rebuild of save/load pipeline: (1) persistContentScope now uses upsert-by-content_key instead of delete-all + insert-all, eliminating the race condition that produced duplicate rows. (2) saveCustom simplified to a single call with no double quest-set save. (3) Quest scope resolution reads _visibility from Supabase row as ground truth. (4) After-save no longer does a full Supabase pull — in-memory state is already correct from upsert, so we just re-merge and re-render immediately. (5) Debug console.log noise removed from save handlers.
Version 1.592 — Content editor: cycle collapse toggle now works. Row click handler was firing even when the toggle button was clicked (stopPropagation was not preventing sibling listener on the same ancestor). Fixed by checking ev.target.closest() in the row handler and returning early for toggle/reorder button clicks.
Version 1.591 — Content scope system simplified for single-developer use. Replaced private/shared/official with Published/Unpublished. Published = shared (visible to all players); Unpublished = private (dev only). Scope dropdown removed from editor header. Each item now has a Published checkbox in its save row. All existing data preserved: private rows = unpublished, shared/official rows = published.
Version 1.590 — Content editor two-part fix: (1) openEditor now pulls from Supabase before rendering the list, so custom cycles appear immediately without needing a manual Pull. (2) Quest save scope now trusts _visibility stamped from the Supabase row as ground truth. Editor scope dropdown defaults to private for all users (admins can promote). Per-form scope selector pre-selects the quest's own scope. These together prevent custom quests from being saved to the wrong (official) bucket.
Version 1.589 — Quest save scope: _resolvedScope now re-reads the live quest object at click time (isBuiltin was a stale closure value from form-render time). Checks _visibility first (stamped from Supabase row visibility column), then only falls back to official for truly built-in content without a custom- setId prefix.
Version 1.588 — Quest save scope fix: quests were being saved to the wrong scope (official) because (1) _visibility was not stamped on quest payloads during mergeCustomContent, so _resolvedScope could not find it; (2) isBuiltin was true for custom quests lacking the _editable flag. Fix: payload now includes _visibility=scope; quests loaded from Supabase get _visibility stamped immediately; _resolvedScope checks _visibility first and skips official for custom- setIds even when user is admin.
Version 1.587 — Reconnected squad picker in quest and grind assignment modals: squad dropdown now populates, auto-selects the active cycle squad, and locks selection when a squad is already assigned to the cycle. Squad change listener restored. The old hard-validation blocks (squad-or-abort) removed — the peg-slot carousel already prevents assigning busy characters.
Version 1.586 — Content editor: saved changes now display immediately. Root cause: mergeCustomContent rebuilt quests with _custom:false (missing _editable flag) and CONTENT_LIB had stale data. Fix: (1) saved quests now include _editable:true so mergeCustomContent preserves _custom:true. (2) After each save, loadContentLibrary pulls fresh data from Supabase before re-rendering the form, ensuring the editor always displays what is actually stored.
Version 1.585 — Quest/grind/gear save deduplication: same fix as cycle rules (v1.582). contentItemsForScope returns duplicates from Supabase; last stale entry was overriding the saved value after mergeCustomContent. Now deduplicates by key (quests: setId+idx, grind: id, gear: name) in each save handler and in mergeCustomContent and loadContentLibrary.
Version 1.584 — Gear content editor: item Type dropdown now has 7 store-aligned types: Weapon, Armor, Consumable, Tool, Magic (Common/Uncommon), Scroll/Book, Potion, Gear (misc), Kit. Store sub-tab filters updated to match: Gear tab routes by new type names; Magic tab includes scroll_book and potion types in Common/Uncommon pools.
Version 1.583 — Store magic tab: rare and very rare items removed from sale. Sub-drawers now show Common and Uncommon only. buildStock pools split into magicCommon (all common) and magicUncommon (5 random uncommon). Rare/legendary items excluded from all store logic.
Version 1.582 — Three fixes: (1) Cycle rules (minAvgSquadLevel, minSquadSize) save correctly — deduplicate questSets by id in ec-save, mergeCustomContent, and loadContentLibrary to prevent stale Supabase duplicates overriding saved values. (2) Store footer redesigned: 2×2 category grid (Gear/Magic top, Assets/Pets bottom); blue sub-drawers appear above grid per category. (3) Test account pill labels use @ HTML entity so Cloudflare CDN cannot obfuscate them when served via GitHub Pages.
Version 1.581 — Three changes: (1) App title h1 ⚔ emoji removed — now plain "Relentless Quest". (2) Seven emoji substitutions in item/quest catalog: Torch 🔦→🕯️, Sending Stones 📡→🪨, Ornithopter 🛥️→🌬️, Sailing Ship 🚢→⛵, Carriage 🪄→🐎, Scout role 🔭→👁️, Slippers of Spider Climbing 👞→🕸️. (3) Content Editor mobile responsive: below 600px the list and form panels become full-screen overlapping layers. Tapping an item adds .ed-form-active class to #ov-editor which slides the list off-screen and brings the form forward. A "‹ Back to list" button (hidden on desktop) returns to the list. Tab bar becomes horizontally scrollable with nowrap. edShowPlaceholder() removes ed-form-active to return to list view. Desktop (>600px) layout unchanged.
Version 1.580 — Downtime badge glitch fix (three-part): (1) Auto-resume bestKey selector now filters dtProgress to only keys with progress > 0 before sorting — previously keys with 0 progress (e.g. faith:Pelor:0 from a long-ago completed track) would win the sort via JS object key ordering, causing a spurious different-type job to queue and add a wrong in-progress badge. (2) _healDowntimeBadges() now checks each badge against _DT_TYPE_BADGE map and removes it only if that type has zero dtProgress AND no active job — multi-track is valid (Celestial + Intel simultaneously = Polyglot Ember + Rune Sleuth both showing). (3) Completion badges (_DT_COMPLETION_BADGES: Skill Savant, Tongue Scholar, Devoted, etc.) are never removed by the healer — they are permanent achievements. The initial over-aggressive fix (strip all in-progress badges except one globally) was reverted as it would have wrongly removed valid multi-track badges.
Version 1.579 — Swap recommendation overhaul: (1) Added _dropScore(dropCi, squadMembers, namedSq) helper that ranks each squad member as a bump candidate across three tiers — redundant hirelings (skills/tools/lang fully covered by non-hirelings in the same squad, score ~0), non-redundant hirelings (score ~10000+), full adventurers (score ~20000+), all secondarily ranked by coverage loss then level. (2) At-capacity unassigned→squad path now attaches dropScore and dropNote to each suggestion. (3) Sort uses delta desc primary, dropScore asc secondary — so when all deltas are equal or negative (the user wants a specific character regardless of coverage), the most expendable member (redundant hireling first) floats to the top as ★ Best. (4) Redundant hireling suggestions show a gold "redundant hireling" badge in the modal row. (5) Swap zone display fix: territory no longer required — squads without a territory fall into an __any__ bucket labelled "Active Squads"; unassigned pool added to every bucket; squadsInSameTerritory() handles both-without-territory match.
Version 1.578 — Swap zone now displays when any locked squad exists (territory no longer required). Previously the zone was hidden unless 2+ squads shared the same territory — but squads without a territory were excluded entirely, so the zone never appeared for single-squad setups. Fix: locked squads without a territory are grouped into an '__any__' bucket; unassigned characters are added to every bucket; zone shows if any bucket has 2+ entries. Territory label shows "Active Squads" for the __any__ bucket. squadsInSameTerritory() updated to return all other locked squads when neither squad has a territory set (both-without-territory match).
Version 1.577 — Territory swap now treats unassigned characters as a virtual swap partner. _unassignedVirtualSquad() builds a virtual squad {name:'__unassigned__', displayName:'Unassigned', memberIdxs:[...]} from all characters not in any named squad. renderSwapZone() adds this virtual squad as a partner for any territory that has at least one locked squad (when unassigned pool is non-empty). Unassigned characters can initiate a swap from the zone; locked-squad characters see "Unassigned" as a swap target. openSwapModal() accepts optional forcedPartner and handles virtual squad on both source and partner sides. Swap execution skips squadObj assignment for __unassigned__ side — removing from a squad without adding to another leaves the character unassigned, and vice versa. Coverage bars and headers use displayName for the virtual squad.
Version 1.576 — Eight-item package: (1) QP balance now refreshed on login — updateQpDisplay() added to both sbPull().then() completion paths so the header shows the correct balance after Supabase data loads. (2) Downtime suggestions exclude in-progress tracks — _dtInProgressSet() checks dtProgress keys and active downtimeJobs; items being worked toward by any party member are moved to the bottom with a 🔄 prefix (still selectable). (3) Quest-assignment gold/reward badges moved from below quest title into the Details card (qcs-1), between the duration line and unmet prereqs. (4) Party screen: characters with active downtime jobs now show a teal 🕯 Downtime badge on their card instead of nothing; downtimeChars set built from S.downtimeJobs where endsAt > now. (5) Quest-map cycle activation with zero squads: routes to a new explanatory modal (ov-squad-intro, z-index 700) describing the squad system and territory mechanics; "Create Squad" closes it and opens the new-squad modal (z-index 710) above the cycle map. Previously-busy-squads path unchanged. (6) Encumbrance drop picker modal (ov-enc-drop) with dropdown. (7) Continue Downtime duration pill modal (ov-dt-dur). (8) Version consolidation.
Version 1.575 — Six-item package: (1) Quest-assignment level prereq now checks only characters assigned to peg slots, not the whole squad/party — falls back to full pool only when no slots are filled yet. (2) Carousel page-number bubbles removed from all three carousels (info card stack, peg-slot carousel, character carousel); left/right arrows now show the page number they navigate to (e.g. ◀ 1, 2 ▶). (3) Downtime target dropdowns now surface franchise coverage gaps: skills/languages/faiths not covered by anyone in the party are prefixed ★ and sorted to the top; the ★ prefix is stripped before saving. (4) Encumbrance-on-transfer dialogue replaced with a proper modal (ov-enc-drop) showing a dropdown of droppable items with weights, replacing the numbered prompt(). (5) Continue Downtime duration replaced with a modal (ov-dt-dur) with 2/4/6/8 hr selectable pills (4 hr default, teal highlight), QP cost shown on confirm button. (6) Version bump consolidating all changes.
Version 1.574 — Four-item package: (1) Task pie interface now requires 3 clicks to fill — each click advances 2 slices (0→2→4→6→0), tooltip updated to reflect "3 clicks = full". (2) Squad banner added to top of quest/adventure page (#adv-squad-banner div, populated in renderAdv) showing active cycle squad emoji, name, member count, and coverage %. Hidden when no squad assigned. (3) Squad roles system: SQUAD_ROLES array defines Captain/Quartermaster/Patrol Leader/Field Medic/Scout with icons, colors, and item effects. ROLE_KITS auto-generated and injected into GEAR at startup (type:kit, price:75gp, wt:5, squadRole field). getCharSquadRole(ci) helper finds a character's role across all squads. renderSqRoles(sq) renders role assignment UI in squad edit modal with dropdowns filtered to squad members (✓ marks kit owners); assignments stored on sq.roles={roleId:ci}. Role badge shown on character cards as colored pill with role icon+label+squad name. (4) Role kits appear in squad inventory collapsible on party screen with character name, assignment status badge (colored if assigned, grey "unassigned" if not), and "0 lb" weight label. Item count in summary includes role kits.
Version 1.573 — QP Manager now accessible to test accounts ([email protected], N=27-37) in addition to app admins. Added isTestAccount() helper that matches emails against /^\d+@test\.com$/. openQpManager() guard updated from isAppAdmin()-only to isAppAdmin()||isTestAccount().
Version 1.572 — Territory Squad Swap system: (1) Cycle editor gains a Territory field (freetext, e.g. "Midlands") — squads assigned to cycles in the same territory can swap members. (2) squadCoverage() formula: counts unique skills/tools/languages/faiths covered by squad members ÷ total possible, returns pct + detail breakdown. (3) Squad tiles now show "N members · X%" coverage and territory label for locked squads. (4) ⇄ Territory Swap Zone appears below squad grid when 2+ locked squads share a territory — shows coverage bars per squad. (5) Select a character then tap the swap zone to open the swap modal; it shows current coverage for all same-territory squads, suggests up to 3 swaps ranked by combined coverage delta (★ Best marked), and executes the swap for costSquadSwap QP (default 10, configurable in QP Manager). Swap is logged and forces an immediate sbPush.
Version 1.571 — Comprehensive item effects editor. Replaced 5-field effect section with full structured system organized into 5 groups: (1) Prerequisite Overrides: overcome terrain, count as skill/tool/language/faith/item prereq. (2) Stat Modifiers: bonus to base stat (STR-CHA), skill check bonus, advantage on skill, level cap bonus, encumbrance bonus. (3) Proficiencies while carried: grant skill/tool/language/weapon proficiency. (4) Franchise/Economic: passive gold per reset, quest gold % bonus, hire cost reduction %, squad size max bonus. (5) Quest/Progression: quest type gold bonus, XP track bonus, QP multiplier, quest duration reduction %. (6) Logistical: unlock terrain type, unlock quest cycle (dropdown of all QUESTSETS), freeform note. All fields optional; empty values omitted from saved effect object.
Version 1.570 — Item Effects added to gear content editor. New "⚙ Item Effects" section below the Bag of Holding checkbox with four fields: (1) Skill Bonus — select a skill + bonus value (e.g. Perception +2); (2) Advantage On — select a skill for advantage on checks; (3) Quest Type Bonus — select a quest type (combat/social/explore/wonder) + bonus value; (4) Counts as Prereq — comma-separated list of prereq names this item satisfies (e.g. "Simple Weapons"); (5) Effect Note — freeform text description of the effect. All fields optional; empty values omitted from saved data. Effect stored on item as item.effect = {skillBonus, advantage, questBonus, prereqAlias[], note}.
Version 1.569 — (1) Quest-character-assignment description card: text enlarged from .88em italic light to 1em normal weight with full ink color and 1.55 line-height. (2) Auto-Assign Grind (50 QP) button now renders directly below the "⚙ Grind Missions — repeatable..." header, using the already-computed _canAutoGrind flag in the grind section. Previously the button only appeared above the toggle when all quests were done; it now also appears in its correct position below the header.
Version 1.568 — Two fixes: (1) Manage Squads button now disabled (with tooltip) when no squads exist. (2) _capstoneCardEarned now treats any card with a dc_cap_ ID as a capstone card regardless of whether _fromCapstone is present — older cards saved before that field existed (like the Rope of Climbing with id dc_cap_rising-reputation_9) were missing the flag and showing unconditionally. SQL needed to backfill existing rows: UPDATE content_items SET data = data || '{"_fromCapstone":true}' WHERE content_type='dashboard_card' AND data->>'id' LIKE 'dc_cap_%' AND data->>'_fromCapstone' IS NULL;
Version 1.567 — Dashboard cards overhaul: (1) Removed hard-coded partyAssets artifact/pet and party NPC auto-tiles — these were creating phantom cards for items not yet earned. Dashboard now only renders editor-managed cards (getDashboardCards) filtered by _capstoneCardEarned. (2) _autoCreateDashCard now stores quest provenance directly on the card object: _sourceSetId, _sourceQuestIdx, _sourceQuestTitle, _sourceCycleName, _unlockedCycles[]. (3) _dashCardClick looks up stored provenance fields first (reliable), falls back to parsing dc_cap_ ID encoding. The detail modal "Received From" and "Cycles Unlocked" panels now use the stored data, not runtime lookups that could fail when setId contains underscores or the card predates the encoding.
Version 1.566 — Dashboard card visibility: added _capstoneCardEarned(card) helper. Auto-generated capstone cards (_fromCapstone:true) now only render if their source quest (decoded from dc_cap_{setId}_{questIdx}) appears in questState.done, setProgress[setId].done, or questCycles history. Manual cards (_fromCapstone not set) always show. This prevents un-earned capstone cards (e.g. Rope of Climbing reward for an uncompleted quest) from appearing in the dashboard. The test NPC card you created manually should be deleted from the Cards editor — manual cards are always visible by design.
Version 1.565 — Franchise Dashboard tiles are now clickable. Every tile opens a card detail modal (ov-dash-card-detail) with a larger version. Quartermaster: shows 3-stat summary bar (total/franchise/party) plus full left-aligned quest log with per-character gold. Reward cards (artifact/pet/NPC/custom): show full description, "Received From" panel (quest title, cycle name, quest description) derived from card ID encoding dc_cap_{setId}_{questIdx}, and "Cycles Unlocked" panel listing any QUESTSETS whose requiredCycleSetId matches the source cycle. NPC contacts show class, species, and contact background. Tiles pass all data via data-dc-* attributes to keep renderDashboard clean.
Version 1.564 — Cards not showing in editor list: edRenderList() had no dashboard branch, so it always wrote empty html to #ed-list when edTab==='dashboard', showing "Nothing here yet." Added a guard at the top of edRenderList that delegates to renderDashboardEditor() and returns early when on the dashboard tab. All paths that call edRenderList() (tab switch, save, delete, scope change) now correctly render the card list.
Version 1.563 — Capstone card auto-creation confirmed working (console log showed Sandy Claws 🦀 saved OK). Added: after _autoCreateDashCard successfully saves, if the content editor is open on the Cards tab it now calls renderDashboardEditor() to immediately show the new card in the list. Diagnostic console logs removed. The card also appears in the Dashboard overlay next time it is opened.
Version 1.562 — Added [DashCard] console logging to _autoCreateDashCard to diagnose why capstone rewards are not auto-generating dashboard cards. Logs: cr object, scope, card being saved, and success/failure of the saveCustom call.
Version 1.561 — Dashboard functions (getDashboardCards, renderDashboard, renderDashboardEditor, _autoCreateDashCard, cardGradient, cardTextColor) were defined OUTSIDE the main IIFE, making contentBucket, saveCustom, esc, S, etc. all undefined. Re-inserted all dashboard functions inside the IIFE before its closing }()); so they share scope with all other app functions. Supabase check constraint also needs updating — see SQL note.
Version 1.560 — Manage Squads crash fixed. renderSqmgrChars() declared _unassigned and _gapSet AFTER the tagHtml block that used them. JS var hoisting meant _gapSet was undefined (not {}) at the point of use, causing "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'Persuasion')" on any character with skills. Moved _gapSet/{} initialization and _gapTags() call to immediately before the tagHtml building block.
Version 1.559 — Two clickability fixes: (1) Manage Squads unclickable — the tagBar (Manage Squads / Available / + Squad) was rendered as the first child of #plist, sitting directly under the sticky Assets/Stats/Recruit bar at z-index:100. That bar physically intercepted clicks. Fix: tagBar is now rendered into a dedicated #party-tagbar div placed in the HTML between the sticky bar and #plist. A new click delegation listener on #party-tagbar handles sqmgr/sqnew/sqedit/ptag actions. Sticky bar z-index lowered from 100 to 10. (2) Cards editor + New button unclickable — renderDashboardEditor() was targeting non-existent #ed-list-items and #ed-list-inner elements, so the + New Card button inside the list was never rendered. The top-level + New button (ed-btn-new) called renderDashboardEditor() which wrote nothing, making it appear broken. Fixed to target the correct #ed-list element. addBtn handler now uses contentBucket() directly with async-safe saveCustom handling.
Version 1.558 — Merges v1.557 fixes plus: Manage Squads button broken — root cause was renderParty() throwing a TypeError on every call. The Continue Downtime IIFE unconditionally called document.getElementById('btn-continue-dt').onclick=... but that button only exists when the bar is visible (has innerHTML). When hidden, getElementById returns null and .onclick throws, silently aborting renderParty before el.innerHTML=html runs — so #plist kept stale content and the Manage Squads button was never rendered. Fix: guarded with if(_dtBtn)_dtBtn.onclick=...
Version 1.557 — Three fixes: (1) Forced sbPush after quest completion, grind completion, and goldSpend ≥50gp — these events call sbPush(false) immediately rather than relying on the 4-second debounce, preventing progress loss if the player closes the tab shortly after collecting rewards. (2) + New button in Cards editor now creates a new dashboard card (was missing the dashboard branch in ed-btn-new handler). Directly writes to contentBucket then calls saveCustom and renderDashboardEditor. (3) _customDashCards fully wired into CONTENT_TYPE_MAP ('dashboard_card') and sbPull content_type switch — cards now round-trip to Supabase correctly and reload on pull. Auto-created capstone cards will also persist properly across sessions.
Version 1.556 — Fix "quest save failed" error when saving a pet capstone reward. _autoCreateDashCard was being called inside the saveCustom .then() chain — its own inner saveCustom call returned a Promise that rejected and propagated up to the outer .catch(), producing the misleading error. Fix: (1) call site wrapped in try/catch so any sync throw cannot bubble; (2) inner saveCustom().then().catch() chain isolated so its rejection never reaches the outer handler. Quest save and card auto-creation now run independently.
Version 1.555 — Dashboard cards two fixes: (1) New Card button was broken — _customDashCards was missing from CONTENT_LIST_MAP (mapped to 'dashCards') and dashCards was not initialized in CONTENT_LIB buckets. getDashboardCards() now reads from bucket.dashCards via contentBucket(). saveCustom/contentItemsForScope now work correctly for dashboard cards. (2) Auto-create card from capstone reward: when a quest editor capstone reward (item/pet/npc) is saved, _autoCreateDashCard() runs and upserts a matching card into the dashboard with pre-filled icon, name, description, and gradient colors matching the reward type (purple for artifacts, green for pets, teal for NPCs). Card id is dc_cap_{setId}_{questIdx} so re-saves update rather than duplicate.
Version 1.554 — Franchise Dashboard: the 📜 Log button on the quest screen now opens a Pinterest-style 3-column card grid overlay. First card is the Quartermaster (gold earned/franchise/party totals; tap to expand full quest log inline). Subsequent cards: any capstone artifact rewards in partyAssets, then custom cards from the editor. Custom cards support icon picker (30 emojis), 3 color pickers with live gradient preview, and a description field. A new 🃏 Cards tab in the Content Editor lets you create/edit/delete these cards. Card data stored in _customDashCards per scope. Card text color auto-selected based on primary color luminance.
Version 1.553 — Two fixes: (1) Auto-assign grind button moved to appear between the quest collapse toggle and the Grind Missions header — only shown when all quests are done and QP balance ≥ 50; removed from grind header row. (2) Store footer completely rebuilt as a 2×2 CSS grid: top row Gear | Pets, bottom row Assets | Magic. Sub-drawers for Gear (⚔/🛡/🎒/🧰), Assets (Mounts/Buildings), Magic (Common/Rare) still animate up. Pets has no sub-drawer. store-tabs CSS changed from flex to grid. pointer-events:all on footer directly. Drawer open check simplified to storeTab===t.
Version 1.552 — Grind auto-assign: an ⚡ Auto-Assign (50 QP) button appears in the Grind Missions header when QP is enabled, balance ≥ 50, and at least one squad member is available. Clicking spends 50 QP and greedily assigns available non-busy squad members (from the active cycle's assigned squad, or full party if none assigned) to as many ready/stretch grind missions as possible. Each mission gets the minimum viable roster (1 adventurer or 2 hirelings). Already-in-progress missions and hard overreach missions are skipped. Each assignment is logged. Falls back to an alert if no missions could be filled.
Version 1.551 — Dev +1 Day now advances all cycles with assigned squads, not just the active one. Secondary cycle pending quests live in S.questState.setProgress[setId].pending — the handler now iterates all setProgress keys that have a squad assigned in S.questCycleSquads and marks their pending quests as done. The log message reports how many secondary-cycle quests were also completed.
Version 1.550 — Five features: (1) Squad manager ribbons show squad-scoped counts when a squad is sorted/selected — format changes to "N/M" (squad count / party total); resets to full-party counts when squad is deselected; ribbon re-renders on squad tile tap. (2) Party screen action bar (Assets/Stats/Recruit) is now position:sticky below the header so it stays visible while scrolling the party list. (3) Faith added as 5th downtime option ("Deepen Faith") — uses WIS modifier, threshold 6, target is any faith the character doesn't already have; on completion sets c.rel to the studied faith and awards "Devotee" badge. (4) "Continue Downtime" button: appears above party list when any characters have in-progress downtime tracks but no active job. Costs 5 QP per character. Prompts for duration in hours, then resumes each character on their most-progressed track. (5) Hireling graduation: class is now auto-selected based on best skill/stat alignment — checks if any existing skill matches a class mandatory skill, otherwise maps best stat to a class (STR→Fighter, DEX→Rogue, CON→Barbarian, INT→Wizard, WIS→Cleric, CHA→Bard).
Version 1.549 — Humdrum Arcana visibility confirmed and fixed. Console showed vis=obscured — the old editor used 'obscured' as the value for "Hidden (only appears when met)". v1.547 renamed 'obscured' to mean "show locked state", breaking existing saves. Fix: render check now treats both 'obscured' and 'hidden' as hide-until-met. Editor simplified to two options: Always Visible / Hidden. Debug log removed.
Version 1.548 — Humdrum Arcana visibility bug: two fixes. (1) Removed duplicate "hidden" option accidentally added in v1.547 — the editor already had the correct Hidden option. (2) cycleGateStatus requiredAsset check now searches character inventories in addition to partyAssets and franchiseStorage. Previously a Rope of Climbing sitting in a character's backpack would not count toward the gate, meaning the gate appeared unmet when it was actually met — or conversely if the item was in storage it would mark the gate met even if the user moved it. Now all three locations are checked consistently. Most likely cause of the display bug: Rope of Climbing is in franchise storage (check Assets screen), making st.ok=true and preventing the hidden condition from firing.
Version 1.547 — Cycle visibility bug fixed. The content editor only offered "Fully Visible" and "Obscured" — there was no way to set true hidden-until-met behavior. The render check (vis==='hidden'&&!st.ok) was correct but unreachable. Added a third option "Hidden — don't show until met" (value:'hidden') to the cycle editor dropdown. To fix Humdrum Arcana: open the content editor, find the cycle, change visibility from Obscured to Hidden, save — it will disappear from the quest map until the Rope of Climbing prerequisite is met.
Version 1.546 — Six fixes: (1) Store footer: pointer-events:all on footer directly (not on children); nav height uses rAF retry loop until nav.offsetHeight is non-zero, fixing mobile tab bar positioning. (2) Squad manager target icons moved from card corner to individual skill/tool/lang/faith tags — a 🎯 appears as a superscript on each specific attribute the unassigned character has that the focused squad currently lacks. (3) QP balance hidden when zero — updateQpDisplay() hides hdr-qp-side and divider when bal===0, same as when QP is disabled. (4) EXOTIC_TOOLS: Poisoner's Kit and Cobbler's Tools replaced with Cartographer's Tools and Navigator's Tools. (5) Downtime badges styled to match XP badges: same border, border-radius, padding, font-size, and font-family; teal color. (6) Downtime badge dialog matches XP format: progress bars per track, percentage fill, all earned badges listed at bottom.
Version 1.545 — (1) Squad manager target icons: when a squad is sorted/focused via the ribbon, unassigned characters who cover at least one attribute the squad lacks get a 🎯 icon in the top-right corner of their card. Hovering shows a tooltip listing the specific gap skills/tools/languages/faith. The icon only appears for unassigned characters (those in another squad are excluded). (2) Party screen "Available" filter button added to the action bar — filters to characters not currently on an active quest or grind mission, i.e. those who can use Downtime. Button toggles (active state shown in green); clears if clicked again. Uses __not_questing__ tag key via charHasTag.
Version 1.544 — Six fixes for character 33 test: (1) Store mobile — store-content gets min-height:0 and -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch so all tabs scroll correctly on mobile. (2) Gear sub-tabs changed from 3 price-based (1–4/5–10/11+) to 4 themed categories: ⚔ Weapons, 🛡 Armor, 🎒 Gear, 🧰 Kits (includes ADVENTURING_GEAR items). (3) Quest-character-assignment carousel arrows repositioned: absolute-positioned on sides of the viewport at vertical center (top:50%/translateY); viewports get margin:0 30-32px to create space. (4) GP display given border-radius:20px to match the QP pill shape. (5) Building assign dropdown: already excluded for non-artifact assets by canAssignReward flag — no change needed. (6) Bag of Holding recognition fixed with _isBagOfHolding() fuzzy helper checking name.includes('bag')&&name.includes('holding') — handles custom-named variants like "Longinus' Carpetbag of Holding". Capstone reward stores item under its actual name in franchiseStorage. Both openSquadNew and sqSave use the helper.
Version 1.543 — Two fixes: (1) Task pie (pct) now persists: pct field added to active_tasks Supabase push row and restored on pull. Requires ALTER TABLE active_tasks ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pct integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 (already run). (2) Capstone Bag of Holding persistence fixed: the bag was awarded to S.partyAssets in memory but the Supabase push may not have completed before the next pull overwrote it. Fix: Bag of Holding is now also added to S.franchiseStorage (string entry) as a durable fallback that travels in the franchise row. awardCapstonReward now calls save() and schedules an explicit sbPush(false) 200ms after the alert is dismissed to guarantee the bag reaches Supabase before any reload.
Version 1.542 — (1) Task pie (pct) now persists to Supabase: pct field added to active_tasks push row and restored in pull. Subtask pct values already travel in the subtasks JSONB column. A pct column is needed in Supabase (see SQL below). (2) Added top-level console.log calls at the entry of openSquadNew() logging partyAssets names/types, franchiseStorage contents, and the hasBag result to diagnose the Bag of Holding recognition issue.
Version 1.541 — (1) Store category tab bar: footer now starts at bottom:0 and is repositioned to nav.offsetHeight in renderStore() so it sits flush on top of the nav regardless of device/safe-area. Scroll spacer updated via requestAnimationFrame after footer renders. (2) franchiseHasBagOfHolding() enhanced with [BagCheck] console logs for each check path to diagnose why the squad creation modal may still show the wrong card. The logic is: storage → inventory → partyAssets (any bag not ownerType=squad).
Version 1.540 — (1) Dev bar now appears below the header, not over it. z-index lowered from 491 to 180 (below header's 220). updateDevBar() reads hdr.offsetHeight and sets bar.style.top dynamically so it always sits flush below the header regardless of its rendered height. (2) Bag of Holding recognition consolidated into franchiseHasBagOfHolding() helper that checks franchiseStorage (strings and objects), character inventories, and partyAssets (any bag whose meta.ownerType is not 'squad'). This correctly handles capstone-awarded bags with type:artifact and meta:{uid} but no ownerType. Both openSquadNew and sqSave now use this logic.
Version 1.539 — Four fixes rolled into store layout update: (1) Dev +1 Day now also auto-resolves all active downtime jobs — sets endsAt to now-1 on each job, calls runDowntimeCompletion(), clears downtimeJobs[], and logs how many were resolved. (2) Faith/religion added as a sixth ribbon in the wishlist (🙏 Faith), pulling from DND.rel; candidate cards already highlighted faith matches. (3) Quest-character-assignment peg slots now correctly preserved when hiring a hireling — the renderQuestAssignmentList wrapper no longer re-inits existing slot keys to null when extraChecked is present; only truly new slot keys are initialized. (4) Bag of Holding received as a capstone reward (type:artifact, no explicit meta.ownerType) is now recognized by the squad creation hasBag check and sqSave bag search — both now use a safe (a.meta&&a.meta.ownerType)||'' pattern instead of assetMeta(a).ownerType which could return undefined on artifact-typed assets.
Version 1.538 — Store layout rebuilt: category tabs now live in a fixed #store-footer positioned directly above the bottom nav (bottom: nav height + safe area). Sub-category drawers animate upward from the top of the tab bar using max-height transition on the same dark background. The content area (#store-content) scrolls independently with a dynamic spacer that adjusts to the footer height so items are never hidden behind the tabs. Active tabs show gold highlight; inactive tabs are muted on the dark bar.
Version 1.537 — Recruiting gold cost mismatch fixed. Root cause: when a max-gold filter was active in the wishlist, renderCandidates re-indexed the filtered results starting at 0, so data-hire="0" on a card pointed to index 0 of the filtered list. The hire handler used that index to look up recruitCandidates[0], which was a different (more expensive) character. Fixed by preserving original recruitCandidates indices when building the filtered display — the hire button now always refers to the correct candidate regardless of which wishlist filters are active.
Version 1.536 — Two bug fixes: (1) Recruit screen no longer traps the user. Root cause: RENDER.party was redirecting to recruit whenever recruitCandidates was non-empty — but candidates were never cleared on close, so navTo(party) always bounced back. Fixed with a _recruitInProgress flag: set when candidates are rolled, cleared on close button click and on successful hire. RENDER.party only redirects when the flag is set. (2) Store panel was displaying on every screen. Root cause: s-store had an inline style="display:flex" which overrode the .scr{display:none} CSS rule, making it permanently visible. Fixed by removing the inline style and adding #s-store.on{display:flex;flex-direction:column} to the stylesheet so it only becomes flex when active.
Version 1.535 — Database schema migration: sbPush and sbPull updated to use proper Supabase columns instead of JSON blobs. franchise table: qp_balance, qp_earned_today, qp_earn_date, franchise_storage, downtime_jobs, quest_log now stored as dedicated columns; settings blob is clean. party table: stats, xp, dt_progress, downtime_badges, coin_purse, is_contact, contact_bg, exotic_trait, former_hireling, owned_items, first_hire now in proper columns; inv array no longer contains __meta__ sentinel string. squads table: squad_name, emoji, color, member_names in proper columns; name column is clean squad name. quest_cycles table: set_id, cycle_num, grind_pending, repeat_counts, set_progress, quest_cycles_history, quest_cycle_squads in proper columns; assigned blob contains only explicit character assignments. All pulls include legacy blob fallbacks for old rows, so existing data is preserved through the migration automatically.
Version 1.534 — Three fixes: (1) hasAnyOwnedBuildingAsset now checks franchiseStorage — buildings purchased via quest modal and stored there unlock franchise storage. (2) Store sub-category drawers are a vertical stack (flex-direction:column) sliding from the right via max-width animation. (3) navTo(party) redirects to recruit screen if candidates were rolled (recruitCandidates or hirelCandidates non-empty).
Version 1.533 — Ten fixes: (1) Hole tap flashes compatible peg cards yellow. (2) Common language is free — always included, not counted against random slots. (3) Dev bar uses position:fixed; body.dev-active adds margin-top. (4) GP gold text / QP white on bright blue. (5) Store two-column layout — category tabs stacked left, sub-drawers in right column. (6) Buildings skip char/squad assignment UI. (7) Slot assignments preserved when hiring hireling from quest modal. (8) qddRefreshInfoPrereqs() called after render to keep ⚠ warnings current. (9) QP balance uses typeof check on save/load. (10) Dev bar mobile fix.
Version 1.532 — Nine features: (1) Dev stat block on party cards. (2) Wishlist scroll preserved on toggle. (3) Max gold ribbon (100–600 gp) filters candidates. (4) Recruit auto-redisplays on return. (5) GP gold text / QP white-on-blue. (6) Store two-column: tabs left, drawers right. (7) WotC species names injects native language (Elvish for Elves, etc.). (8) Quest nav scrolls to first assignable quest. (9) questPrereqHtml shows "✗ none available" in red for prereqs no unassigned squad member can cover.
Version 1.531 — Dev mode shows STR/DEX/CON/INT/WIS/CHA stat block on party screen character cards between inventory and action buttons. Six stats with label, value, and modifier. Only visible in dev mode.
Version 1.530 — Seven improvements: (1) Wishlist selected pills sort to front of ribbon; deselecting returns to natural position. (2) Store drawers inline below each tab (each drawer directly below its tab in DOM). (3) QP toggle: hdr-qp-side.hdr-sw-active gets bright blue background (#1a6fd4). (4) questReadiness uses active cycle squad members for checks. (5) Exit Dev in bar via flex-wrap fix on #dev-bar.active. (6) Level-up buttons removed from prereqs info card (live on peg cards only). (7) Buildings forced to franchise storage in buy flow; buildingReq checks both partyAssets and franchiseStorage.
Version 1.529 — Eight changes: (1) Login pills reset to 27–[email protected]. (2) Wishlist clear button added. (3) Wishlist scroll position preserved on deselect. (4) Store drawers inline below each tab. (5) Store search box searches all items across tabs. (6) Exit Dev inline in dev bar. (7) Exit Dev no longer asks for confirmation. (8) ⚡ Recall button on in-progress quests: spends 25 QP, removes from pending, releases characters, no gold reward.
Version 1.528 — Six improvements: (1) Wishlist expanded to Skills/Tools/Languages/Classes/Species ribbons with 🎯 target icons for missing party attributes. (2) Dev bar constrained to max-width:480px. (3) QP spam guard: tasks <10 chars or no vowel in first 5 chars skip QP vesting. (4) Bottom nav only hides on mobile keyboard (visualViewport shrink <75%), not desktop focus. (5) Dev bar Ledger button navigates to gold log screen. (6) Capstone reward bug fixed: _alreadyDone checked before pushing to done[], so reward now actually fires.
Version 1.527 — Five fixes: (1) Subtask pie recalculates on subtask add. (2) Wishlist ribbon added to recruit screen: sqmgr-style ribbons for Skills/Tools/Languages/Classes/Species. 🎯 target icon on attributes not in party. Candidate cards highlight matching attributes green. (3) Subtask checkbox pie updates in-place to reduce nav flicker. (4) Storage Shed found by store-link (BUILDINGS added to lookup; subtab auto-set to exp-buildings). (5) Peg card level-up clickable: qcb-lvup intercepted before data-peg-ci handler.
Version 1.526 — Level-up button added to quest character peg cards: shown when quest has explicit level requirement and character falls short. Uses existing qcb-lvup handler. Only for non-hirelings who are not busy.
Version 1.525 — Five improvements: (1) Hire ribbon applies to hirelings too (focusNewHireling scope fix). (2) Quest description card: line-clamp removed; vehicle/building requirements get 🛒 Purchase button opening expedition store tab. (3) Checking a subtask auto-increments parent task pie (done/total×6 formula). (4) Hireling level-up cost raised to 200 gp at all four cost sites. (5) Recruit candidate cards show roster count badges on skill/tool/lang/faith/class/species pills; zero counts highlighted green.
Version 1.524 — Four quest modal improvements: (1) Hire modal (3s modal → 2s ribbon, ⚔ Next Quest + 👤 View buttons). (2) GP/QP fully mutual — clicking GP hides it, shows QP only; clicking QP hides it, restores GP. Dev bar adds ⏩ +1 Day button (completes quests, +400 gp, +20 QP). (3) Unmet level requirement shows level-up buttons for squad members below requirement instead of Recruit link. (4) Item prerequisites in questPrereqCheck now only count items carried by characters in peg slots — storage no longer satisfies item prereqs.
Version 1.523 — Hire ribbon becomes a modal: centered modal with 🎉, character name, "⚔ Next Quest" and "👤 View [Name]" buttons. After 3s shrinks to a green ribbon for 2s then fades. Clicking overlay or either button dismisses immediately. Hire applies to hirelings from quest panel too. encumbranceRelinquishPrompt: instead of blocking a transfer, user can relinquish an equal-or-heavier item to franchise storage. Default quest duration set to 30s. speciesNames defaults to true. Store: Adventuring Gear on second row. Store search box added. Dev bar Exit Dev inline.
Version 1.522 — Six gold diagnostic [Gold] console.log statements added at every S.gold assignment point to help trace the gold reset bug.
Version 1.521 — Login pills updated to 20–[email protected] with tester1 password. Register button restored.
Version 1.520 — Login: test user dropdown replaced with pill buttons 20–[email protected]. Register button restored.
Version 1.519 — QP switch: GP only shown on launch. QP side revealed on first task creation with shooting-star animation (12 emoji stars fly from random positions toward the QP badge). revealQpWithStars() awards 20 QP and switches pill to QP highlighted. _qpRevealed flag prevents repeat. Login panel: dropdown of test users 1–10 auto-fills email+password. "+ New Test User" button creates next sequential account, adds to dropdown, auto-logs in.
Version 1.518 — Login credentials updated to [email protected] / tester6.
Version 1.517 — Login panel: email/password pre-filled with [email protected] / tester5.
Version 1.516 — QP enabled by default (cfg.qp.enabled=true). 20 QP welcome bonus awarded on fresh account creation. QP side of switch visible when enabled.
Version 1.515 — GP/QP pill replaced with a proper two-sided toggle switch: hdr-gp-side and hdr-qp-side as separate spans with hdr-sw-active class for highlighting. QP side hidden until revealed. Divider hidden when only one side shows.
Version 1.514 — GP/QP pill rebuilt again as flex track with proper overflow:hidden clipping. Both panels are 100% wide inside a 200%-wide track; sliding to QP uses translateX(-50%) to bring QP exactly into view.
Version 1.513 — Store tab order: Adventuring Gear first (default open with 1–4 gp sub-drawer), Magic second. storeTab defaults to "adventuring".
Version 1.512 — Two quest fixes: (1) Characters held busy until gold is claimed — pending entries hold characters regardless of whether the quest timer has expired; released only when the user collects the reward. (2) qddAssignToSlot eviction bug fixed — incoming character (ci) is removed from any other slot they occupied, but the previous occupant (prev) is no longer evicted from all their slots.
Version 1.511 — Level requirement error message improved: if a character on another quest would satisfy the level requirement, message now reads "Level N+ required — your highest eligible adventurer is on another quest. Wait for them to return." instead of generic "Party level N+ (highest: 0)".
Version 1.510 — GP/QP pill rebuilt as flex track (two 100%-wide panels side-by-side, clipped by overflow:hidden). Sliding to QP moves the track translateX(-50%). Clicking GP panel triggers switch to QP; clicking QP switches back.
Version 1.509 — QP switch introduced in header pill: GP and QP share a two-sided toggle. GP side shown by default (gold, active). QP side (⚡, blue) hidden until QP is enabled. Periodic auto-peek every 25–60s briefly shows QP then returns to GP. Tapping manually toggles. QP enabled by default. 20 QP awarded on first task creation with a shooting-star animation. _qpRevealed flag prevents repeat.
Version 1.508 — Pie checkbox interaction fix: subtask checkbox moved outside the label element so checking a subtask no longer simultaneously triggers the checkbox AND the pie toggle. Pie button intercepts click before the label.
Version 1.507 — Several polish changes: (1) Squad "Filter by" button added to the collapsible squad inventory bar on the party screen, between the Edit button and the squad icon. When that squad is the active filter, it reads "✕ Unfilter". Filtering by squad shows full character cards (not collapsed), with the squad tag rendered as a plain pill rather than the crimson active style. Top action bar gains "✕ Clear filter" when any filter is active. (2) "Sever" renamed to "RIF" with title="Reduction in Force" tooltip; confirmation dialog and log entries updated to use reduction-in-force language. (3) Exploration XP badge rank 3 changed from "Ranger" to "Woodsman". (4) Task and subtask progress pie: a 28px SVG circle to the left of each task and subtask title, divided into sixths. Starts white/empty; each tap fills the next sixth clockwise from 10 o'clock in a different color (amber, blue, green, red, purple, orange). Seven taps cycles back to empty. No border, no text — just the pie. Value stored as step 0–6 on t.pct / s.pct.
Version 1.506 — Several polish changes: (1) Squad filter buttons added to the collapsible squad inventory bar on the party screen — "Filter by" button between Edit and the squad icon; shows "✕ Unfilter" when that squad is already the active filter. When filtering by squad, full character card content is shown (skills, languages, inventory, tools — not collapsed), and the squad tag renders as a plain pill rather than the crimson active style. Top action bar shows "✕ Clear filter" when any filter is active. (2) "Sever" button on character cards renamed to "RIF" with title="Reduction in Force" tooltip. Confirmation dialog reads "Reduction in Force: release [Name] from the roster for X gp?"; gold log reads "RIF: [Name] released"; blocked-action alerts say "cannot be released" in all cases. Internal data-a="sever" action name unchanged. (3) Exploration XP badge rank 3 changed from "Ranger" to "Woodsman" (Scout → Pathfinder → Woodsman → Wayfarer → Master Explorer).
Version 1.505 — Three auto-assign improvements: (1) Candidate pool restricted to members of the active cycle's squad — characters from other squads can no longer be pulled in by auto-assign. Falls back to full party if no squad is assigned. (2) canAutoFill() simulation: dry-runs the full greedy covering algorithm (same squad restriction, same busy-character filter, adventurers then hirelings) without mutating state. Returns true only if all prereq slots can be covered by available squad members. (3) Auto button (⚡ 1 QP) is grayed out (disabled + opacity 0.4) whenever canAutoFill() returns false, with an updated tooltip. Re-enables automatically when conditions change (hireling hired, slot filled manually, etc.).
Version 1.504 — Quest-map Activate button: if a squad is already assigned to the cycle, skip the squad-picker modal and instead close the map, navigate to the Adventure screen, and scroll to the first unfinished quest (first assign button, falling back to first collect button) with a smooth scroll. Squad picker still opens normally when no squad is assigned yet.
Version 1.503 — Auto-assign algorithm rewritten. Old algorithm: greedy slot-by-slot, placed any available character into any slot regardless of whether they covered it, used hirelings. New algorithm: Phase 1 — find the minimum set of adventurers to cover all prereq slots using a set-cover greedy (pick the candidate covering the most remaining unfilled slots, assign them to all those slots, repeat until no adventurer covers any remaining slot or all slots filled). Phase 2 — fill support slots with remaining adventurers who meet the level requirement. Hirelings are excluded from auto-assign entirely (they require deliberate manual hire via the quest panel). Characters who cover no prereq slots (e.g. high-level generalists) are placed in support, not forced into prereq slots they cannot fill.
Version 1.502 — averageLevelForIndices now excludes hirelings (level 0) from the calculation. Hirelings are temporary workers, not adventurers, and their level-0 status was unfairly dragging down the party average used for cycle gate requirements (minAvgSquadLevel checks). A squad of four level-5 adventurers plus two hirelings now correctly reports an average level of 5, not 3.3.
Version 1.501 — Comprehensive squad system cleanup and quest-cycle integration: Squad inventory now appears for ALL squads on the party page (not just those with assets), showing member count and item count. Squad-owned assets are excluded from the Purchased Assets panel and Franchise Storage. Bag of Holding gets party:true so it routes through the asset-assignment modal when purchased. Enrollment adds bag directly to partyAssets (not franchiseStorage). Meta mutation set directly on asset object. cycleSquadMap() auto-cleans stale entries for disbanded squads on every call. Disband also explicitly removes cycle assignment. squadIsLockedByCycle() helper gates roster changes in sqmgrAssignChar, sqSave, and the squad edit modal — cycle-locked squads show a crimson notice with checkboxes disabled. Quest hire panel checks squad capacity and shows "Squad full" instead of Hire button when at 10; on hire, new hireling is auto-added to the active cycle squad if a spot exists. Stale squad badge removed from quest-map modal. Version numbering resumed at 1.501.
Version 1.500 — Over-selection behaviour changed to next-to-last-out at all three points: the newest selection always succeeds; it is the second-newest (the one immediately before it) that gets evicted. (1) Squad Manager: assigning to a full squad removes the most recently added existing member (splice at length-1), animates them sliding left, then adds the new character. (2) Squad creation checkboxes: checking an 11th box slides away the second-to-last checked box (allChecked[length-2]). (3) Mercenary toggles: tapping when at the limit evicts list[length-1] with a scale-fade animation, then adds the new selection.
Version 1.500 — Three party-screen and squad-modal fixes: (1) Squad inventory items on the party page no longer show a re-assignment dropdown — items show icon, name, and a "squad only" label. The only way to release a squad bag is to disband the squad. (2) The returning-customer enrollment button on the used-car-salesman card changed from green-on-green (btn bgn + background:forest) to gold-on-white (background:gold-dark, border:gold) so it is legible. (3) Squad filter/tag chips removed from the party page; replaced with a slim two-button bar (Manage Squads + New Squad). Edit buttons moved into the left side of each collapsible squad inventory summary bar, so squads with assets can be edited directly from their inventory header. Also: next-to-last-out eviction at all three selection-limit points (squad manager, creation modal, mercenary toggles).
Version 1.499 — Squad bag-of-holding assignment now uses the same mechanism as the store: when a new squad is created and a Bag of Holding exists in franchise storage, it is moved from S.franchiseStorage into S.partyAssets with meta {ownerType:'squad', ownerRef:squadName} — exactly as the buy modal does for squad-assigned items. Removed the previous bagStorageIdx approach. Party screen now shows collapsible squad inventory sections (coloured by squad colour, labelled by squad emoji + name) above the character list for any squad that has items assigned to it. Each item row shows the asset icon, name, and a re-assignment dropdown (same as the party assets panel) so players can move items between squads, characters, storage, or buildings from the party view.
Version 1.498 — Three squad improvements: (1) On squad creation, an unclaimed Bag of Holding from franchise storage is automatically assigned to the new squad via bagStorageIdx (only one squad per bag — first-come basis). (2) Assigning a character to a full squad (10 members) in the Squad Manager now silently evicts the longest-standing member (LIFO: shift from index 0) with a slide-left fade animation, then adds the new member. Same behaviour in the squad creation members list — checking an 11th box animates the first checked item sliding away before it is unchecked. No warning or redirect. (3) Mercenary hire skill/tool/language toggles: hitting the max no longer shows an alert. Instead, the oldest selection is silently evicted (list.shift()) with a scale-down fade animation on that button, then the new selection is added.
Version 1.497 — Three squad manager improvements: (1) 10-member limit now shows a smooth warning overlay ("Squads hold a maximum of 10 adventurers / Saving your progress…") that fades in over 1.4s then fades out before transitioning to the Squad Manager, instead of the abrupt immediate switch. (2) Character cards in the Squad Manager now show ALL of a character's skill proficiencies by default. When a ribbon filter is active, they instead show the relevant proficiency type (all skills with the filtered one highlighted, all languages, all tools+weapons, or faith) — so the filter context is always visible on the card. (3) If the franchise already owns a Bag of Holding, the enrollment modal shows an alternative card in the voice of a used-car salesman, offering squad re-registration for 50 gp ("skip the certified package, sew your own tabards…"). The standard 500 gp brochure is shown when no Bag of Holding exists anywhere in franchise storage or character inventories.
Version 1.496 — Squad modal redesigned as a 3-card horizontal swipe with dot indicators: (1) Card 0 — for new squads, shows an enrollment brochure listing branded livery, charter document, territory registration, bulletin board listing, ID badges, and a free Bag of Holding (400 gp value) for the 500 gp enrollment fee. Paying the fee awards the Bag of Holding to franchise storage and advances to card 1. For edits, card 0 shows the name field and a Next button. (2) Card 1 — identity: squad name, 16 emoji symbols, 10 colour swatches. (3) Card 2 — member selection with a running (N / 10) counter; hitting 11 saves progress and redirects to the Squad Manager. Save button on card 2 commits all changes. Disband button visible only in edit mode. openSquadNew() and openSquadEdit() replace inline modal setup code at all call sites.
Version 1.495 — Fixed squad emoji and color not persisting across devices. Root cause: sbPush only serialised squad name and member names into the Supabase squads table row; emoji and color were silently dropped. sbPull reconstructed squads without them, so every pull reset squads to default values. No SQL migration needed — fixed by packing emoji and color into the name string as a __sqmeta__{...} suffix (alongside the existing __members__ suffix). sbPull now strips and parses __sqmeta__ before __members__, restoring both fields. Old rows without __sqmeta__ fall back to defaults gracefully.
Version 1.494 — Squad manager improvements: (1) Unassigned tile added as the permanent 8th cell in the 4×2 squad grid (grid now supports up to 7 squads + always-present None tile). Selecting a character then tapping None calls sqmgrUnassignChar, removing them from all squads. Tapping None with no character selected sorts unassigned members to the top. (2) Single skill ribbon replaced with four filter ribbons (⭐ Skills, 🙏 Faith, 🗣 Lang, 🔧 Tools). Each is a horizontally scrollable row with a fixed label at left. Tools ribbon merges DND.weapons and DND.tools. Chips only appear for values at least one party member holds. Selecting any chip filters the character list; selecting the same chip again clears the filter. Only one ribbon filter is active at a time.
Version 1.493 — Fixed emoji and color pickers in squad modal being completely unresponsive. Root cause: btn-sqmgr-resort was removed from the overlay HTML during the v1.490 redesign but its addEventListener call remained in the script. Since script execution is sequential, the null-reference crash on that line prevented all subsequent event handler registrations from running — including the ov-sq-emojis and ov-sq-colors click handlers. Fixed by removing the dead addEventListener call. The remaining guarded reference (in sqmgrReSort flash) was already safe.
Version 1.492 — Fixed emoji and color pickers in the Add/Edit Squad modal being unresponsive. Root cause: SQUAD_COLORS was updated to desaturated values in v1.489 but the static HTML color buttons still had the old saturated hex codes. sqResetEmojiColor matched data-c values against the saved squad color, found no match, and left no button selected — making the picker appear stuck. Fixed: color picker HTML buttons updated to match SQUAD_COLORS exactly. Also added a fallback in sqResetEmojiColor so if no button matches the saved value, the first button is selected, preventing a permanently empty picker state.
Version 1.491 — Squad manager rebuilt around a peg-and-hole interaction model: (1) Fixed 4×2 squad tile grid at top — up to 8 squads, empty cells shown as dashed placeholders. Tapping a squad tile while no character is selected sorts that squad to the top of the list; tapping the same tile clears the sort. (2) Horizontally scrollable skill filter strip below the grid — one chip per D&D skill showing member count. Tapping a skill filters the character list to members with that proficiency. (3) Character cards show Name (bold), Class, Level, and their 2 best skills ranked by the governing ability score (e.g. Persuasion ranks by CHA, Athletics by STR) — using c.stats assigned at character creation/graduation. (4) Peg-and-hole assignment: tap a character card to select it (gold ring highlight, hint bar updates), then tap a squad tile to assign. Character immediately moves to that squad's color. SKILL_ABILITY mapping covers all 18 D&D skills to their governing ability.
Version 1.490 — Squad manager redesigned around a paint-bucket model: (1) Fixed 3×3 grid of squad tiles at the top — up to 8 squads + an "Unassigned" tile, empty placeholders fill remaining cells. Tiles show emoji, name, and a selected indicator (white ring). (2) Swipe system removed. Instead: tap a tile to select it as the active paint bucket, then tap any character row to assign them — the row flashes to the new color and the icon animates, then the list re-sorts after a 520ms beat to group same-squad members together. (3) Character zone is fully scrollable (flex:1 overflow-y:auto), grid is fixed above it. Hint bar between grid and chars updates to show the active bucket name. Filter system removed in favor of the paint interaction.
Version 1.489 — Squad manager overhaul: (1) Squad background colors desaturated for readability. (2) Squad creation member list now hides characters already assigned to other squads — only unassigned characters and the squad's current members appear. (3) Character rows enlarged: padding 10×12, emoji icon 1.3em, name/class/skill all .9em, name bold. (4) Squad cards reorganised into a 2-column Pinterest grid with emoji, name, and member count only — no character rows inside. (5) Squad cards are now filter toggles: tapping a card filters the character list below to show only that squad's members. An "Unassigned" card (with count) also appears in the grid. Tapping the active card clears the filter. Filter resets when the manager is opened.
Version 1.488 — Two fixes: (1) Quest character assignment: tapping ✕ on a filled peg-slot (hole-remove) now clears ALL slots that character occupies, matching the behavior of removing the character card from the carousel. Previously only the tapped slot was cleared, leaving the character silently filling other slots. (2) Squad manager redesigned as a flat list: all party members appear in one scrollable list with icon and background color indicating squad membership — no grouping headers. Characters with no squad show a neutral row. Swiping left cycles to the next squad (or back to unassigned after the last), swiping right goes the other direction. The legend at the top shows squad chips with name, emoji, member count, and an edit button. One-squad-per-character rule enforced: swipe removes from current squad before adding to the next.
Version 1.487 — Squad management overhaul: (1) Squad creation/edit modal now includes emoji selector (16 options: ⚔🛡🗡🏹🔥❄⚡🌿💀🐺🦅🌙☀⭐🎯🪄) and color picker (10 rich background colors). Emoji and color saved to squad object. (2) New Squad Manager overlay (⚔ Manage button on party screen): 2-column Pinterest-style grid of squad cards, each showing emoji, name, member count, and a compact single-row card per member (emoji · name · class · level · primary skill). (3) Swiping a member row left/right cycles the character through available squads with an animated emoji/color transition and re-renders on completion. (4) Re-sort button at top reorders S.party so squad members are grouped together, updating all memberIdxs to match the new indices.
Version 1.486 — Three quest modal fixes: (1) Auto-advance to page 2 of the peg-hole carousel now only triggers if all 4 non-support prerequisite slots on page 1 are filled — quests with fewer than 4 prereqs no longer trigger the slide.
